entities.name: log_strength.py
entities.context: Python CLI script for logging strength training sessions to a SQLite database (workouts.db)

entities.name: workouts.db
entities.context: SQLite database storing workout/strength training session data

entities.name: SQLite
entities.context: Database engine used for persistent workout data storage

entities.name: Strength Training
entities.context: Exercise modality tracked via structured logging with RPE, sets, reps, duration, and soreness

entities.name: RPE
entities.context: Rate of Perceived Exertion — subjective intensity metric used to grade workout difficulty (scale referenced in script)

entities.name: Upper Body Circuit
entities.context: One workout type category tracked in the system (e.g. push-ups, rows)

entities.name: Exercise Logging
entities.context: Structured tracking of individual exercises with sets, reps, and deviation notes per session

entities.name: Next-Day Soreness
entities.context: Post-workout soreness logged after sessions as a recovery/adaptation signal

entities.name: Deviation Notes
entities.context: Free-text field capturing how a session differed from the prescribed routine (e.g. skipped or substituted exercises)

relationships.context: Script reads/writes all session data to the SQLite database
RPE is a required parameter when logging a strength session
Script accepts structured JSON of exercises (name, sets, reps) per session
Script has a subcommand to log soreness against a previous session ID
Script captures deviations from planned routine as optional metadata
RPE is the primary intensity metric for strength sessions
Upper body is one of the workout type categories in the system
Database file uses SQLite as its storage engine
